channel.title: Security on DevOpsTales
channel.description: Recent content in Security on DevOpsTales

item.title: Kubernetes Egress Gateway Solutions - Complete Comparison & Recommendations
item.description: This final post brings together everything from the egress gateway series with a comprehensive comparison of all 8 solutions, decision matrices for different scenarios, cost analysis, and practical migration strategies.

item.title: Kubernetes Egress Gateway with Squid Proxy
item.description: Squid is a mature, full-featured HTTP proxy and caching server that can be deployed on Kubernetes for egress control. This post explores using traditional proxy technology for modern Kubernetes egress requirements with access control, caching, and comprehensive logging.

item.title: Kubernetes Egress Gateway with Custom Envoy Proxy
item.description: Deploying a custom Envoy proxy as an egress gateway provides maximum flexibility and control over outbound traffic. This post explores building a self-hosted egress solution using Envoy’s advanced features without the complexity of a full service mesh.

item.title: Kubernetes Egress Gateway with Monzo Egress Operator
item.description: Monzo’s Egress Operator is an open-source Kubernetes operator that automates the provisioning and management of AWS NAT Gateways for Kubernetes egress traffic. This post explores a cloud-native approach to egress gateway management with Kubernetes-native CRDs.

item.title: Kubernetes Egress Gateway with Antrea
item.description: Antrea is an open source Kubernetes CNI based on Open vSwitch that provides advanced networking features including Egress Gateway - completely free and open source. This post explores Antrea’s egress capabilities as a true open source alternative.

item.title: Kubernetes Egress Gateway Options with Cilium
item.description: Controlling outbound traffic from your Kubernetes cluster is critical for security, compliance, and audit requirements. This post covers Cilium Egress Gateway - a powerful open source solution built on eBPF.

item.title: Send pfSense Logs to Wazuh
item.description: Centralizing pfSense firewall logs in Wazuh SIEM provides critical security visibility and threat detection capabilities. This updated guide for 2026 covers pfSense 2.7.x/2.8.x and Wazuh 4.8+ with proper syslog formatting and decoder configuration.

item.title: Kubernetes Certificate Rotation
item.description: In this post I will show you how you can rotate your Kubernetes Engine Certificates.

item.title: Best Practices for Keeping Kubernetes Clusters Secure
item.description: Kubernetes offers rich configuration options, but defaults are usually the least secure. Most sysadmins don’t know how to secure a Kubernetes cluster. So this is my Best Practice list for keeping Kubernetes Clusters Secure.

item.title: Implementing Mutual TLS (mTLS) with Traefik Ingress Controller: Per-Ingress Configuration
item.description: Mutual TLS (mTLS) adds a critical security layer to your Kubernetes applications by requiring clients to authenticate with certificates—not just passwords or tokens. This guide covers per-ingress mTLS configuration with Traefik Ingress Controller, using Traefik’s native TLSOption CRD for fine-grained control over which services require client certificate authentication.

item.title: Implementing Mutual TLS (mTLS) with NGINX Ingress Controller: Per-Ingress Configuration
item.description: Mutual TLS (mTLS) adds a critical security layer to your Kubernetes applications by requiring clients to authenticate with certificates—not just passwords or tokens. This guide covers per-ingress mTLS configuration with NGINX Ingress Controller, giving you fine-grained control over which services require client certificate authentication.
